title: rosa villosa subsp. alpicola
intro: Rosa villosa subsp. alpicola, also known as the alpine sweet rose, is a fragrant and flowering plant species within the Rosa villosa complex. It is a type of wild rose with medicinal properties, primarily used for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The alpine sweet rose has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin problems, wounds, and respiratory conditions, due to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. Herbal preparations of Rosa villosa subsp. alpicola include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
